test.name: Virginia / West Virginia boundary change
test.description: Tests whether the skill explains that an apparent birthplace conflict between Virginia and West Virginia is caused by the 1863 state boundary change, not by the ancestor moving. Tests boundary-change knowledge and record-jurisdiction guidance.

input.user_message: My ancestor was listed as born in Virginia in the 1860 census, but the 1870 census says West Virginia. Did they move?

judge_context: Must explain that West Virginia separated from Virginia in 1863 during the Civil War — the ancestor almost certainly did not move
Must clarify that county boundaries also changed frequently, not just state boundaries, and research should focus on the county level
Must mention that records (land, probate, court, tax lists) remain in the original county jurisdiction regardless of which state that county later fell into
Must direct the researcher to consult historical boundary maps (e.g. Historical Map Works or the FamilySearch locality guide) to identify the correct county before searching any record repository
Should mention specific record types held at county level: land and property records, tax lists, probate and court records
